## Why IV shows up at all

An instrument gives you variation in treatment that is not contaminated by the same confounders that bias the raw comparison.

::: {.teacher-only}
Prompt the room for one policy variable that plausibly shifts treatment but does not directly move the outcome.
:::

## The two checks students need first

1. Relevance: the instrument must move the treatment.
2. Exclusion: the instrument must not directly move the outcome except through the treatment.

## Reading the design

When those two checks are plausible, IV replaces a biased comparison with variation that behaves more like an experiment.
